# Shell fluency вЂ” from lost at the prompt to querying text like a database рџџў

**Goal.** Find your feet at the shell, learn to program it, wield grep/sed/awk on real text вЂ” then discover that composing those tools was relational algebra all along, meet the sets underneath it, rebuild `ls` and the pager you read its output with from bash builtins, and finally ask the shell for a *number* and find out it cannot divide 1 by 3. The first three steps are Matt Might's track (he names the survival guide as the prerequisite to bash-by-example); an equivalent Carpentries + Robbins track is a side-quest.

**Audience:** complete beginners welcome; the last two steps ask a little more  
**Effort:** вЏ± half-day+  
**Prerequisites:** none

| # | Lab | You'll learn | Checkpoint (observable) |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | [`UNIX_novice_survival_guide`](../UNIX_novice_survival_guide/) | navigate, pipe, redirect, and get help without a cheat-sheet. | in the `~/unix-survival` sandbox, reproduce the guide's `find \| grep READ` and rename `baz`в†’`bar`, unaided. |
| 2 | [`shell-intermediate-programming-by-example`](../shell-intermediate-programming-by-example/) | variables, loops, functions, arguments вЂ” programming the shell, not just typing at it. | run `demo.sh` в†’ it prints `5!=120`; then write your own script with a loop + a function that processes argv. |
| 3 | [`UNIX-sculpting-text-regex-grep-sed-awk`](../UNIX-sculpting-text-regex-grep-sed-awk/) | wield the GNU grep/sed/awk trio on real text. | run `demo.sh` (byte-identical on Debian + Alpine); then extract and reformat fields from a log in a single `awk` line. |
| 4 | [`UNIX-relational-algebra-sql-in-the-shell`](../UNIX-relational-algebra-sql-in-the-shell/) рџџЎ | those same tools ARE relational algebra: `cat`=в€Є, `awk`=Пѓ, `cut`=ПЂ, `comm -23`=в€’, `join`=в‹€ вЂ” and Unix never shipped a Cartesian product, so Might writes one in 15 lines of bash. | run `demo.sh` в†’ `PASS: all 9 relational identities hold`, i.e. Might's hand-rolled bash, coreutils, and `sqlite3` agree on the same relations; then find why `equijoin -t` silently returns 0 rows. |
| 5 | [`UNIX-set-operations-in-the-shell`](../UNIX-set-operations-in-the-shell/) рџџЎ | the SETS under those relations, and the three algorithm families that build them вЂ” merge (`comm`), count (`uniq -c`), hash (`awk`) вЂ” plus why `comm` and `sort -n` silently disagree about what 'sorted' means. | run `demo.sh` в†’ `PASS: all 28 set identities hold`; then run `treasure-hunt.sh` в†’ it recovers Google's 2008 puzzle answer `7830239` with `uniq -d`, and shows the article's own `comm + sort -n` recipe losing it. |
| 6 | [`UNIX-ls-without-ls`](../UNIX-ls-without-ls/) рџџЎ | you've been QUERYING with these tools вЂ” now REBUILD one: `ddls` reimplements ls from bash builtins + GNU stat + tput (one `stat --printf` fork per file, `printf %(...)T` instead of `date`, an insertion sort instead of `sort`), and the only honest test for a reimplementation is a byte-for-byte diff against the original. Its own header lies three times (the `dd` in the name is branding); demo.sh pins every lie and every divergence. | run `demo.sh` в†’ `PASS: all 32 checks hold` вЂ” including `ddls -la == ls -la` byte-identical; then explain why `--color=never` colors anyway (initialization order) and why `-t` disagrees with ls about same-second files (seconds vs nanoseconds).<br>рџ”Ћ auto-checkable в†’ marker `PASS: all 32 checks hold` рџ–ҐпёЏ |
| 7 | [`UNIX-less-without-less`](../UNIX-less-without-less/) рџџЎ | then rebuild the screen you read it on: `ddpager` is a working less (search, filters, a file ring, follow mode) from bash builtins + dd + tput. Two mechanisms carry it: dd's stderr byte-count vs bash's NUL-stripping `$( )` as a binary detector, and the raw-mode illusion вЂ” `stty raw` turns ICRNL and ISIG off, yet Enter scrolls and Ctrl-C exits 130, because bash's `read -n1` swaps its own termios in during every read. Testing a TUI honestly means a real pty and human-speed keystrokes. | run `demo.sh` в†’ `PASS: all 28 checks hold` вЂ” the pager driven through a real pty by `drive-pager.py`; then run the dd-reader experiment and explain why dd sees byte 13 where bash's read saw Enter.<br>рџ”Ћ auto-checkable в†’ marker `PASS: all 28 checks hold` |
| 8 | [`UNIX-floating-point-arithmetic-in-bash`](../UNIX-floating-point-arithmetic-in-bash/) рџџЎ | you have mastered these tools on *text* вЂ” now ask the shell for a *number* and watch `$((1/3))` return **0**, silently. Two people refused to accept that: Cyrus does long division in 12 lines of integer `$(( ))`, and Wood's `shellmath` implements + в€’ Г— Г· in pure Bash, dodging the `$( )` fork to beat `bc`. The reframe: it's a *bash* limit, not a *shell* one вЂ” zsh and ksh93 have had float `$(( ))` all along. | run `demo.sh` в†’ `PASS: all 25 checks hold` (bc == awk == pure-bash div == shellmath); then explain why the same run asserts that `0.1+0.2 == 0.3` is **true** for `bc` and **false** for `awk` вЂ” and why *both* are right. |

### Optional side-quests

- [`shell-novice-workshop`](../shell-novice-workshop/) вЂ” the Carpentries (CC-BY) full-day equivalent of steps 1вЂ“2, by different authors.
